summary: Login components silently relay end-user credentials to author-controlled Supabase project
details: The package's exported authentication components (LoginPage, useAuth) hardcode a Supabase project URL (https://vmicscahrnzpmhagztmx.supabase.co) and anon JWT in dist/supabase/client.js. Any application that uses the advertised LoginPage or useAuth hooks sends its end-users' email, password, and password-reset requests to the author's Supabase backend rather than the integrator's own. There is no environment-variable override or configuration mechanism on the browser client — the destination is baked in. Whether the cause is an accidental publish of a private app scaffold or deliberate, the structural shape is silent-relay: caller-supplied secret data (end-user credentials) is unconditionally routed to a destination the package author controls, with no consent surface for the integrator or their users.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
